title: RESY EUNIS habitat classifications
text: following R packages required run example vignette:
code: library(readr) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(sf) # library(vegdata)  # not needed: taxon names are cleaned natively (see below) library(RESY)

title: RESY EUNIS habitat classifications
text: example data include species data vegetation surveys sites data information location surveys. First, vegetation surveys: Second, sites data:
code: data_species <- read_csv(system.file("extdata", "data_example_species.csv", package = "RESY"), skip = 0) data_species #> # A tibble: 3,580 × 3 #>    PlotObservationID cover species                                              #>    <chr>             <dbl> <chr>                                                #>  1 HU32                0.1 Dryopteris filix-mas (L.) Schott                     #>  2 HU32                0.1 Cephalanthera longifolia (L.) Fritsch                #>  3 HU32                0.5 Prenanthes purpurea L.                               #>  4 HU32                0.5 Anemone nemorosa L.                                  #>  5 HU32                0.5 Epipactis helleborine (L.) Crantz subsp. helleborine #>  6 HU32                0.5 Sorbus aucuparia L. subsp. aucuparia                 #>  7 HU32               87.5 Fagus sylvatica L.                                   #>  8 HU32                3   Rubus hirtus Waldst. & Kit.                          #>  9 HU32                3   Abies alba Mill.                                     #> 10 HU32                3   Oxalis acetosella L.                                 #> # ℹ 3,570 more rows  # clean names with RESY's native cleaner: drops author strings, keeps ranks data_species$species <- resy_clean_names(data_species$species) # data_species$species <- vegdata::taxname.removeAuthors(data_species$species) data_sites <- read_csv(    system.file("extdata", "data_example_sites.csv", package = "RESY")    ) |>    select(-"cover") |>    st_as_sf(coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) data_sites #> Simple feature collection with 200 features and 1 field #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 9.809499 ymin: 42.32483 xmax: 12.08963 ymax: 44.32464 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> # A tibble: 200 × 2 #>    PlotObservationID            geometry #>  * <chr>                     <POINT [°]> #>  1 JZ37               (9.93798 44.32464) #>  2 FR49              (11.09287 43.91546) #>  3 PT45              (11.65339 43.09104) #>  4 ZH63              (10.53301 43.73897) #>  5 TF93              (10.40239 44.23978) #>  6 KG68              (10.40203 44.24087) #>  7 QJ27               (10.40635 44.2413) #>  8 JN90              (10.40602 44.24296) #>  9 ZM18              (10.66071 44.12734) #> 10 BQ20               (10.6589 44.12456) #> # ℹ 190 more rows

title: RESY EUNIS habitat classifications
text: vegetation surveys example dataset Tuscany Italy.  see part Italy, mainly Tuscany Mediterranean specific Tyrrhenian Sea. dots locations vegetation surveys. islands.

title: RESY EUNIS habitat classifications
text: First, prepare sites species data resy_harmonize_eunis() three warnings: column Altitude (m) missing covered resy_harmonize_eunis(), provide vignette("Get altitude data"). NAs ecoregions (Ecoreg) country (Country) columns. vegetation surveys islands covered base map.
code: parsed <- RESY::resy_load_expert() outcome <- RESY::resy_harmonize_eunis(    data = data_sites,    source_crs = 25832,    run_taxonomy = TRUE,    species_data = data_species,    parsed = parsed,    run_coast_dunes = TRUE,    coast_buffer = 5000    ) #> Warning in RESY::resy_harmonize_eunis(data = data_sites, source_crs = 25832, : #> "Altitude (m)" is missing. See mapsforeurope.org for a raster source. #> Warning in RESY::resy_harmonize_eunis(data = data_sites, source_crs = 25832, : #> NA values in "Ecoreg": some sites are outside the ecoregion base map. #> Warning in RESY::resy_harmonize_eunis(data = data_sites, source_crs = 25832, : #> NA values in "Country": some sites are outside the country base map. #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries outcome_sites <- tibble(outcome$sites) outcome_species <- tibble(outcome$species_checked)

title: RESY EUNIS habitat classifications
text: Chytrý M, Řezníčková M, Novotný P et al. (2024) FloraVeg.EU – online database European vegetation, habitats flora. – Applied Vegetation Science 27, e12798. https://doi.org/10.1111/avsc.12798 Bruelheide H, Tichý L, Chytrý M, Jansen F (2021) Implementing formal language vegetation classification expert systems (ESy) statistical computing environment R. – Applied Vegetation Science 24, e12562 https://doi.org/10.1111/avsc.12562 Chytrý M, Tichý L, Hennekens SM et al. (2020) EUNIS Habitat Classification: expert system, characteristic species combinations distribution maps European habitats. – Applied Vegetation Science 23, 648–675. https://doi.org/10.1111/avsc.12519 Mucina L, Bültmann H, Dierßen K et al. (2016) Vegetation Europe: hierarchical floristic classification system vascular plant, bryophyte, lichen, algal communities. – Applied Vegetation Science 19(Suppl. 1), 3–264.https://doi.org/10.1111/avsc.12257

title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: RESY implements Expert System (ESy) framework vegetation classification (Bruelheide 1997; Chytrý et al. 2020). ESy file encodes three sections: Taxonomy – species aggregations synonyms Taxon groups – species assemblages referenced classification conditions Vegetation types – logical formulas combine group thresholds named habitat types package can work ESy-compliant classification. vignette uses two bundled systems: EUNIS (European Nature Information System habitat types; FloraVeg.EU) Apennine-test (simplified test system Italian Apennines) EUNIS specifically, many formulas also require geographic columns (Ecoreg, Country, Coast_EEA, Dunes_Bohn) sites header table. preparation steps covered vignette("EUNIS").
code: library(readr) library(dplyr) library(RESY)

title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: resy_classify() expects observation table three columns: minimal header table (one row per plot) required even geographic conditions used. must contain plot ID column observation table.
code: data_species <- read_csv(   system.file("extdata", "data_example_species.csv", package = "RESY"),   show_col_types = FALSE ) |>   rename(TaxonName = species, Cover_Perc = cover)  glimpse(data_species) #> Rows: 3,580 #> Columns: 3 #> $ PlotObservationID <chr> "HU32", "HU32", "HU32", "HU32", "HU32", "HU32", "HU3… #> $ Cover_Perc        <dbl> 0.1, 0.1, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 87.5, 3.0, 3.0, 3.0, 0… #> $ TaxonName         <chr> "Dryopteris filix-mas (L.) Schott", "Cephalanthera l… header <- data_species |>   distinct(PlotObservationID) |>   as.data.frame()

title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: resy_available_classifications() lists schemes versions available (# function resy_available_classifications() gives esy files bundled package far. Either expand delete function. add custom expert system, use resy_add_classification(). resy_load_expert() parses bundled classification resy_parsed_expert object, used taxonomy checking. resy_classify() loads expert internally scheme/version.
code: resy_available_classifications() |> select(scheme, version) #>          scheme    version #> 1 Apennine-test 2026-06-27 #> 2         EUNIS 2025-10-03 parsed_apennine <- resy_load_expert(scheme = "Apennine-test")

title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: resy_check_taxonomy() maps observation names Section 1 expert system (canonical names + synonyms). classification-specific: name may resolve one system another.
code: tax_apennine <- resy_check_taxonomy(   obs    = data_species,   parsed = parsed_apennine,   col    = "TaxonName" ) cat("Apennine-test — matched:", sum(tax_apennine$matched),     "/ unmatched:", sum(!tax_apennine$matched), "\n") #> Apennine-test — matched: 1009 / unmatched: 0

title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: resy_classify() evaluates membership formulas returns resy_result object. Pass scheme select bundled expert system; loaded automatically.
code: res_apennine <- resy_classify(   obs    = data_species,   header = header,   scheme = "Apennine-test" )

title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: browse type hierarchy loaded system, use resy_view_expert().
code: tree_filled <- resy_expert_tree(parsed_apennine, fill = TRUE) print(tree_filled) #> <resy_expert_tree> 5 node(s), 2 top-level #> F Forest #>   FB Beech-fir montane forest #> N Non-forest #>   NG Nardus acidic grassland #>   NS Sub-Mediterranean scrub and woodland

title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: resy_eval_type() shows conditiosn type evaluated.
code: resy_eval_type(res_apennine, t = "FB") #> FB    Beech-fir montane forest #>  #> (<#TC Beech-forest-trees GR 15> AND <#TC Beech-forest-herbs GR 10>) #>  #> (col2 & col3) #>  #>   expressions                  #> 2 #TC Beech-forest-trees GR 15 #> 3 #TC Beech-forest-herbs GR 10

what: Long table of candidates for all plots
title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: resy_candidates() extracts ranked classification results. Use top_n limit number types returned per plot.
code: cand_apennine <- resy_candidates(res_apennine, top_n = 3) head(cand_apennine) #>    plot_id   type priority priority_rank #>     <char> <char>    <ord>         <int> #> 1:    AM30      F        2             1 #> 2:    AN57      F        2             1 #> 3:    BE71      F        2             1 #> 4:    BE71     FB        5             3 #> 5:    BK34      N        2             1 #> 6:    BK34     NS        3             2

title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: resy_eval_plot() prints full evidence one plot: species matched, group conditions fired, vegetation-type formulas evaluated TRUE.
code: # Replace "AN57" with a PlotObservationID present in your data resy_eval_plot(res_apennine, p = "AN57") #> Plant observations for plot AN57 : #>     PlotObservationID Cover_Perc               TaxonName        group_names #>                <char>      <num>                  <char>             <char> #>  1:              AN57        0.1 Gymnocarpium dryopteris               <NA> #>  2:              AN57        0.1   Athyrium filix-femina               <NA> #>  3:              AN57        0.1     Prenanthes purpurea Beech-forest-herbs #>  4:              AN57        0.1      Dryopteris expansa               <NA> #>  5:              AN57        0.1      Polypodium vulgare               <NA> #>  6:              AN57        0.5       Oxalis acetosella Beech-forest-herbs #>  7:              AN57        0.5        Sorbus aucuparia               <NA> #>  8:              AN57        0.5              Abies alba Beech-forest-trees #>  9:              AN57       62.5         Fagus sylvatica Beech-forest-trees #> 10:              AN57       37.5     Vaccinium myrtillus   Nardus-grassland #> Possible types of plot "AN57" (135): F #> Priorities of these types: 1  #> Classified as: F

title: Classifying vegetation surveys with RESY
text: EUNIS geographic enrichment (ecoregion, country, coast), see vignette("EUNIS"). validate ESy text file importing, use resy_validate_esy().

title: Altitude data
text: First, illustrate altitude data example dataset.
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(here) library(sf) library(terra) library(tidyterra)

title: Altitude data
text: Load altitude data area example. load example plots.
code: altitude <- terra::rast(   system.file("extdata", "data_example_altitude.tif", package = "RESY") ) altitude #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 63, 168, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 0.0005555556, 0.0005555556  (x, y) #> extent      : 10.69171, 10.78504, 44.12662, 44.16162  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) #> source      : data_example_altitude.tif #> name        : eurodem #> min value   :     771 #> max value   :    1927 data_sites <- readr::read_csv(   system.file("extdata", "data_example_sites.csv", package = "RESY"),   show_col_types = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::select(PlotObservationID, Longitude, Latitude) |>   sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) |>   sf::st_transform(terra::crs(altitude))

title: Altitude data
text: show map altitude data plots  © EuroGeographics 2026, Istituto Geografico Militare (IGM), Italy; Licence
code: ggplot() +   tidyterra::geom_spatraster(data = altitude) +   tidyterra::scale_fill_whitebox_c(     palette = "high_relief",     na.value = "lightblue"     ) +   geom_sf(data = data_sites, color = "red", size = 1) +   labs(fill = "Altitude (m)") +   theme_minimal() altitude_values <- terra::extract(altitude, terra::vect(data_sites)) data_sites <- data_sites |>   mutate("Altitude (m)" = altitude_values$eurodem) data_sites #> Simple feature collection with 200 features and 2 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 9.809499 ymin: 42.32483 xmax: 12.08963 ymax: 44.32464 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> # A tibble: 200 × 3 #>    PlotObservationID            geometry `Altitude (m)` #>  * <chr>                     <POINT [°]>          <dbl> #>  1 JZ37               (9.93798 44.32464)             NA #>  2 FR49              (11.09287 43.91546)             NA #>  3 PT45              (11.65339 43.09104)             NA #>  4 ZH63              (10.53301 43.73897)             NA #>  5 TF93              (10.40239 44.23978)             NA #>  6 KG68              (10.40203 44.24087)             NA #>  7 QJ27               (10.40635 44.2413)             NA #>  8 JN90              (10.40602 44.24296)             NA #>  9 ZM18              (10.66071 44.12734)             NA #> 10 BQ20               (10.6589 44.12456)             NA #> # ℹ 190 more rows

title: Design-Decisions
text: foster collaborative development package discussions/decisions scope, general structure functionality packages documented discussed .

title: Design-Decisions
text: toolbox classify plant species co-occurrence matrices, aka vegetation plots formalised, rule based -called “expert systems”. ideas rule-based assignments go back 1990th application gained momentum development expert system European Habitat Type (EUNIS). package aims general implementation expert-system logic, including tools develop check new expert systems. Expert systems use indicator species groups, abundances, presence (relative) amount species indicator group. Furthermore, header data like geographic location can used identify e.g. ecoregion, country plot located coast, e.g. needed EUNIS expert system. expert system plot assignment European EUNIS habitat types used example throughout package additional helper functions prepare data EUNIS classification included.

title: Design-Decisions
text: version 1 package aims simple generic implementation CRAN submission intended, means helper data (e.g. prepare data EUNIS classification) small enough test driven development desirable better standardisation, including json format expert-system file, completeness available formal rules tests checking correct execution rules intended shiny app development, curation test expert systems included specialised helper functions EUNIS classification integrated named used internally

title: Vegetation type expert classification system
text: goal RESY use -called expert files assign vegetation plots types. function resy_classify() core function classify vegetation plots, , depending rules need include also site data. Insert figure . Perhaps Europe

code: install.packages("RESY") library("RESY") # install.packages("devtools") remotes::install_github(    "https://gitlab.uni-rostock.de/loe/publications/r-esy",    dependencies = TRUE    ) library(RESY)

what: Apply solver-required formula transformations to a parsed expert system — .resy_transform_formulas
title: Apply solver-required formula transformations to a parsed expert system — .resy_transform_formulas
text: Takes raw aggregations, groups, membership formulas formula names (produced either text JSON section parsers) applies transformations needed solver: #T$ completion, GR NON insertion bare ##D/##C/##Q expressions, EXCEPT completion #SC conditions, -prefix expansion.

title: Apply solver-required formula transformations to a parsed expert system — .resy_transform_formulas
code: .resy_transform_formulas(   aggs,   groups,   membership.formulas,   membership.formula.names )

title: Apply solver-required formula transformations to a parsed expert system — .resy_transform_formulas
text: aggs Named list species aggregations (Section 1). groups Named list species groups (Section 2). Names must carry `"### "`, `"##D "`, `"$$C "` `"$$N "` prefix. membership.formulas Character vector raw Section 3 formula strings. membership.formula.names Character vector formula name strings format `"<priority><10 chars padding><code> <description>"`.

title: Apply solver-required formula transformations to a parsed expert system — .resy_transform_formulas
text: list elements `aggs`, `groups`, `membership.expressions`,   `group.defs`, `formulas`, `membership.priority`.

what: Add a new classification to the RESY store — resy_add_classification
title: Add a new classification to the RESY store — resy_add_classification
text: Validates copies ESy expert file (`.txt` `.json`) RESY classification store becomes available [resy_load_expert()] [resy_classify()]. file validated [resy_validate_esy()] anything written. validation fails function stops message lists every error found, can fix file try . Pass `validate = FALSE` skip validation (recommended).

title: Add a new classification to the RESY store — resy_add_classification
code: resy_add_classification(   file,   scheme,   version,   location = c("user", "package"),   overwrite = FALSE,   validate = TRUE )

title: Add a new classification to the RESY store — resy_add_classification
text: file Path ESy expert file (`.txt` `.json`). scheme Scheme name store (e.g. `"MyClassification"`). version Version string store (e.g. `"2026-07-05"`). location store: `"user"` (default, recommended) `"package"` (requires writable library). overwrite Logical; overwrite existing classification. Defaults `FALSE`. validate Logical; run [resy_validate_esy()] storing. Defaults `TRUE`. Set `FALSE` certain file valid.

title: Add a new classification to the RESY store — resy_add_classification
text: named list paths files written: `txt` Path stored `.txt` file, `NULL` JSON input. `json` Path stored `.json` file.

Missing geographic columns filled automatically calling internal EUNIS geo-assignment helpers.

See mapsforeurope.org for a raster source. #> Warning: The column "Coast_EEA" is missing. #> Warning: The column "Dunes_Bohn" is missing. #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 5 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 701327 ymin: 5364375 xmax: 701327 ymax: 5364375 #> Projected CRS: ETRS89 / UTM zone 32N #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   PlotObservationID Ecoreg Country Country_ID Ecoreg_name                        #>               <int>  <dbl> <chr>   <chr>      <chr>                              #> 1                 1    686 Germany DE         Western European broadleaf forests #> # ℹ 1 more variable: geometry <POINT [m]>

what: Check plot data for EUNIS classification requirements — resy_check_eunis
title: Check plot data for EUNIS classification requirements — resy_check_eunis
text: Validates input data frame sf object meets structural requirements EUNIS habitat classification. pure check — reports issues without modifying data. Use [resy_harmonize_eunis()] actually enrich prepare data. Checks performed: `PlotObservationID` column present. Coordinates present can transformed EPSG:25832. `Altitude (m)` column present free `NA`. `Ecoreg` `Country` columns present (need assigned). `Coast_EEA` `Dunes_Bohn` columns present coastal/dune     classification relevant.

NULL, uses latest available version scheme. location look scheme/version "user" "package". id_col Optional name plot id column. NULL, tries PlotObservationID, PlotID. mc Number CPU cores use.

what: Remove taxonomic authorities from scientific names — resy_clean_names
title: Remove taxonomic authorities from scientific names — resy_clean_names
text: Strips author citations scientific plant names keeping taxonomic structure expert-system names depend , names different taxonomic backbones resolve canonical names synonyms expert system. genus species epithet always kept. Infraspecific rank markers (subsp., var., f., ...) kept together epithet, ssp. abbreviation normalised subsp.. Aggregate collective markers (agg., aggr., coll., s.l., s.str., s.lat.) stand alone kept place, including trail name following epithet ("Taraxacum officinale agg.") follow infraspecific epithet ("Aconitum napellus subsp. firmum s.l."). sensu lato sensu stricto qualifiers (forms s.l., s. l., sens. lat., sensu lato, stricto equivalents, compact s.s.) normalised s.l. / s.str. kept; sensu <author> concept attribution (example "aggr. sensu Buser") dropped, match bare form expert system uses canonical name. hybrid sign (ASCII x multiplication sign) kept: nothospecies ("Salix x rubens"), leading nothogenus ("x Ammocalamagrostis baltica"), hybrid formula joining two taxa ("Betula pendula x pubescens", "Elytrigia repens x Leymus arenarius"), hybrid never collapsed onto one parents. Names fewer two words returned unchanged.

title: Remove taxonomic authorities from scientific names — resy_clean_names
code: resy_clean_names(x)

title: Remove taxonomic authorities from scientific names — resy_clean_names
text: x character vector scientific names, optionally carrying author citations (example "Fagus sylvatica L.").

title: Remove taxonomic authorities from scientific names — resy_clean_names
text: character vector length x author citations   removed taxonomic structure preserved. NA values preserved.

title: Remove taxonomic authorities from scientific names — resy_clean_names
code: resy_clean_names(c(   "Fagus sylvatica L.",   "Epipactis helleborine (L.) Crantz subsp. helleborine",   "Senecio ovatus (G.Gaertn., B.Mey. & Scherb.) Willd.",   "Taraxacum officinale agg.",   "Festuca ovina s. l.",   "Alchemilla vulgaris aggr. sensu Buser",   "Mentha x verticillata aggr." )) #> [1] "Fagus sylvatica"                          #> [2] "Epipactis helleborine subsp. helleborine" #> [3] "Senecio ovatus"                           #> [4] "Taraxacum officinale agg."                #> [5] "Festuca ovina s.l."                       #> [6] "Alchemilla vulgaris aggr."                #> [7] "Mentha x verticillata aggr."

what: Build the classification hierarchy of an expert system — resy_expert_tree
title: Build the classification hierarchy of an expert system — resy_expert_tree
text: Turns type definitions resy_expert object (Section 3, "Group definitions") hierarchy. fill = FALSE (default) tree uses codes expert defines: definition nests another code code longest prefix among codes present, EUNIS file codes R, R1 R12 nests R12 R1 R, codes present prefix stay top-level. levels invented, keeps hierarchy faithful files define flat code list. fill = TRUE intermediate group levels implied code grammar reconstructed: code split ancestor path code_path, ancestor expert define added structural group node (marked synthetic column). EUNIS yields single tree rooted formation groups (M, N, Q, R, ...) every type proper depth.

title: Build the classification hierarchy of an expert system — resy_expert_tree
code: resy_expert_tree(   expert,   section = 3L,   fill = FALSE,   code_path = .resy_code_path,   names = NULL )

title: Build the classification hierarchy of an expert system — resy_expert_tree
text: expert resy_expert object resy_read_expert, read entries = TRUE (default). section Integer; section number holding type definitions. Defaults 3, EUNIS-ESy convention. fill Logical; TRUE, reconstruct intermediate group levels implied codes. Defaults FALSE. code_path Function mapping one code character vector ancestor codes root including code . Used fill = TRUE. default splits code one level per character ("Q11" becomes c("Q", "Q1", "Q11"), "MA1" becomes c("M", "MA", "MA1")), matches EUNIS code grammar. names Optional code--name lookup used label nodes expert leaves unnamed (typically synthesised group levels). Either named character vector (names codes) data frame whose first two columns code name. resy_eunis_names returns official EUNIS habitat names form. Defaults NULL (external names).

title: Build the classification hierarchy of an expert system — resy_expert_tree
text: object class resy_expert_tree: data frame one row   per node columns id, parent (NA top-level   nodes), code, description, priority, expression,   level (1 top-level nodes), leaf, synthetic   (TRUE reconstructed group node expert define)   name_source ("expert", "lookup" "").   print method renders indented text tree.

title: Build the classification hierarchy of an expert system — resy_expert_tree
code: f <- system.file("extdata/classifications/Meadow-ESy-2025-01-18-2025.txt",                  package = "RESY") if (nzchar(f)) {   expert <- resy_read_expert(f)   print(resy_expert_tree(expert))            # codes as defined   print(resy_expert_tree(expert, fill = TRUE)) # with group levels filled in }

Use [resy_check_eunis()] first verify input data meets requirements running function.

Required `data` plain data frame; ignored `data` already `sf` object. run_taxonomy Logical; `TRUE`, runs [resy_check_taxonomy()] `species_data`. species_data data frame column `species`. Required `run_taxonomy = TRUE`. parsed `resy_parsed_expert` object [resy_load_expert()]. Required `run_taxonomy = TRUE`. run_coast_dunes Logical; `TRUE`, assigns `Coast_EEA` `Dunes_Bohn` flags via spatial intersection. coast_buffer Numeric buffer metres coastline proximity (default 5000).

text: Loads parsed expert-system object ready use [resy_classify()]. `expertfile` given takes precedence `scheme`/`version`. file format detected extension: `.json` Parsed directly via internal JSON parser (preferred). `.txt` Parsed via legacy text parser. `expertfile` supplied, function looks `expert.json`, `expert.txt` (order) `inst/extdata/classifications/<scheme>/<version>/` installed package.

title: Load a classification — resy_load_expert

text: expertfile Optional path `.json` `.txt` file. scheme Classification scheme name (default `"EUNIS"`). version Version identifier. `NULL`, newest available version used.

title: Load a classification — resy_load_expert
text: list class `resy_parsed_expert`.

what: Parse a structured JSON expert-system file — resy_parse_json
title: Parse a structured JSON expert-system file — resy_parse_json
text: Reads structured JSON expert-system definition file internal `resy_parsed_expert` format consumed [resy_classify()]. JSON file must following top-level keys: `metadata` Object `scheme` `version` (strings).     Optional: `description`, `source_file`. `synonyms` Object mapping canonical name (key) array     accepted spelling variants / author-cited synonyms (value). empty     array means canonical name known aliases. `groups` Object mapping species-group name (key, including     solver prefix `"### "`, `"##D "`…) array canonical     member species. `rules` Array objects, `priority` (character 0–9     letter), `code` (string), `description` (string), `expression`     (string using formula syntax, e.g.     `"<#TC Beech-forest-trees GR 15>"`). Keys starting `_`     (e.g. `"_comment"`) silently ignored.

title: Parse a structured JSON expert-system file — resy_parse_json
code: resy_parse_json(path)

title: Parse a structured JSON expert-system file — resy_parse_json
text: path Path `.json` expert-system file.

title: Parse a structured JSON expert-system file — resy_parse_json
text: list class `resy_parsed_expert`, identical structure   output [resy_load_expert()].

what: Read an expert-system file losslessly — resy_read_expert
title: Read an expert-system file losslessly — resy_read_expert
text: Parses expert-system definition file (EUNIS-ESy .txt format compatible expert systems) structured tree preserves source byte--byte. lossless counterpart resy_parse_expert. resy_parse_expert rewrites formulas solver's internal form (therefore one-way), resy_read_expert retains file's structure verbatim inspection conversion formats.

title: Read an expert-system file losslessly — resy_read_expert
code: resy_read_expert(file, entries = TRUE)

title: Read an expert-system file losslessly — resy_read_expert
text: file Path expert-system .txt file (example official release EUNIS-ESy-2025-10-03.txt). entries Logical, default TRUE. TRUE, known section also carries structured entries view content (aggregations, groups, definitions). FALSE raw lines kept, faster goal pure round-trip semantic view needed.

title: Read an expert-system file losslessly — resy_read_expert
text: object class resy_expert: list components meta File-level metadata: line_terminator (CRLF       LF) trailing_newline (whether file ends       terminator). preamble Character vector lines first       SECTION header. sections list, source order, section objects.       number, name, header_line, body_lines,       end_line (NA section matching       SECTION N: End), trailing (lines section's       end next section end file), ,       entries = TRUE section type known, entries:       structured view section content.

what: Validate an expert classification file — resy_validate_esy
title: Validate an expert classification file — resy_validate_esy
text: Checks ESy expert file (`.txt` `.json`) structural syntactic problems. assess ecological correctness. rules : **Errors** (always fatal — file imported): required sections / top-level keys must present ,     `.txt`, correct order (Section 1 < 2 < 3). Every vegetation type must non-empty code whitespace. Every vegetation type must formula / expression. Every formula must contain least one membership condition     <...>. Brackets inside formulas must balanced ((), [],     {}). Priority must single character 0-9, -Z     -z. Group keys Section 2 / "groups" must start     recognised prefix (###, ##D, #TC, #SC,     $$C, $$N). **Warnings** (errors strict = TRUE): Duplicate vegetation type codes. Group names referenced formulas defined. Dangling logical operators (//    start end formula). Legacy relational operator (deprecated JUICE 7.0). `.txt`: relational operators found outside <...> angle     brackets. `.json`: missing empty metadata.scheme /     metadata.version. Empty description vegetation type.

title: Validate an expert classification file — resy_validate_esy
code: resy_validate_esy(path, strict = FALSE, verbose = TRUE)

title: Validate an expert classification file — resy_validate_esy
text: path Path `.txt` `.json` expert file. strict Logical; `TRUE`, treat warnings errors. verbose Logical; `TRUE` (default), print one-line summary.

title: Validate an expert classification file — resy_validate_esy
text: list : `ok` `TRUE` errors found. `errors` Character vector error messages. `warnings` Character vector warning messages. `meta` Named list: `path`, counts groups vegetation       types defined.

title: Render an expert-system hierarchy and open it in a browser — resy_view_expert

code: resy_view_expert(   expert,   section = 3L,   fill = TRUE,   open_level = 1L,   title = NULL,   names = NULL,   name_tag = NULL,   browse = interactive() )

title: Render an expert-system hierarchy and open it in a browser — resy_view_expert
text: expert Either resy_expert object (resy_read_expert) resy_expert_tree (resy_expert_tree). section Integer; definition section use expert resy_expert. Defaults 3. Ignored resy_expert_tree. fill Logical; reconstruct intermediate group levels (see resy_expert_tree). Defaults TRUE view, page shows single tree rooted formation groups. Ignored resy_expert_tree, already built. open_level Integer; nodes level shallower start expanded. Defaults 1 (top groups open, contents collapsed). title Page title; defaults section name. names Optional code--name lookup nodes expert leaves unnamed (see resy_expert_tree); pass resy_eunis_names EUNIS. Ignored resy_expert_tree, already built. name_tag Optional short label shown beside names supplied names (example "EUNIS"), mark apart expert's descriptions. Defaults NULL (tag). browse Logical; open file browser. Defaults interactive().

title: Render an expert-system hierarchy and open it in a browser — resy_view_expert
text: path written HTML file, invisibly.

title: Write an expert-system hierarchy to a self-contained HTML page — resy_write_expert_html
text: expert Either resy_expert object (resy_read_expert) resy_expert_tree (resy_expert_tree). file Path HTML file write. section Integer; definition section use expert resy_expert. Defaults 3. Ignored resy_expert_tree. fill Logical; reconstruct intermediate group levels (see resy_expert_tree). Defaults TRUE view, page shows single tree rooted formation groups. Ignored resy_expert_tree, already built. open_level Integer; nodes level shallower start expanded. Defaults 1 (top groups open, contents collapsed). title Page title; defaults section name. names Optional code--name lookup nodes expert leaves unnamed (see resy_expert_tree); pass resy_eunis_names EUNIS. Ignored resy_expert_tree, already built. name_tag Optional short label shown beside names supplied names (example "EUNIS"), mark apart expert's descriptions. Defaults NULL (tag).

title: Write an expert-system hierarchy to a self-contained HTML page — resy_write_expert_html
text: file path, invisibly.

title: Write an expert-system hierarchy to a self-contained HTML page — resy_write_expert_html
code: f <- system.file("extdata/classifications/Meadow-ESy-2025-01-18-2025.txt",                  package = "RESY") if (nzchar(f)) {   out <- file.path(tempdir(), "expert-tree.html")   resy_write_expert_html(resy_read_expert(f), out) }

title: Internal string utilities — trim.trailing
code: trim.trailing(x)

title: RESY 0.3
text: prepare_eunis() split resy_check_eunis() (pure validation) resy_harmonize_eunis() (geographic enrichment + taxonomy). check_taxonomy() → resy_check_taxonomy() check_format() → resy_check_data() resy_validate_expert() → resy_validate_esy()

title: RESY 0.3
text: resy_parse_expert(), resy_parse_json(), resy_show_vegtypes() now internal (unexported). Use resy_load_expert() single entry point loading expert system. Geographic helpers (check_ecoregions, check_country, check_coast_dunes, check_coordinates) consolidated internal functions internal_eunis_geo.R .resy_assign_* / .resy_check_* naming convention. RDS format support removed. Expert systems imported .txt stored .json. resy_available_classifications() longer returns expert_rds column. resy_expert_path() format argument now c("json", "txt") .

title: RESY 0.3
text: resy_check_eunis() — validates plot data EUNIS structural requirements harmonization, returning list(ok, errors, warnings). New general vignette vignette("RESY") covering classification EUNIS Apennine-test expert systems without EUNIS-specific geographic steps.

title: RESY 0.2
text: Added resy_read_expert(), resy_write_expert(), resy_expert_tree(), resy_write_expert_html(), resy_view_expert(), resy_eunis_names() lossless reading, writing, hierarchical inspection expert-system files. Added resy_parse_json() direct parsing structured JSON expert files. prepare_eunis() now returns named list sites species_checked. new parsed argument required run_taxonomy = TRUE. check_taxonomy() signature updated: obs, parsed, col replace former species argument. resy_available_classifications() now discovers package-bundled user-added classification files. resy_expert_path() gains "json" supported format. Bug fixes documentation updates throughout.
